title: Provincial Nominee Program: Non-Express Entry Process  Get nominated by a province or territory  - Canada.ca
content:
Provincial Nominee Program: Non-Express Entry Process

## Get nominated by a province or territory
To apply as a provincial nominee, you must first
- decide in which province or territory you want to live in Canada
- apply for a nomination under one of the province or territory’s non-Express Entry streams
They will review your application based on
- their immigration needs
- whether you meet the minimum requirements for the non Express Entry stream
- whether you really plan to live there
Once you get a nomination from a province or a territory, you can apply for permanent residence.

## Find a province or territory for nomination
To be nominated by a province or territory, follow the instructions on their website and contact them directly.
Select a province or territory to learn about their PNP programs
- Newfoundland and Labrador
- Northwest Territories
- Prince Edward Island
Nunavut does not have a provincial nominee program. Learn more about how to immigrate to Nunavut .
Quebec does not have a provincial nominee program. Learn more about Quebec’s immigration programs
Print all pages in this section
